Momenta

Momenta is a reactive UI framework for Rust focused on simple, fine-grained reactivity and JSX-like authoring through the rsx! macro.

Primary resources
- Documentation site: https://elcharitas.github.io/momenta/
- Repository: https://github.com/elcharitas/momenta
- Crate: https://crates.io/crates/momenta

Project summary
- Core authoring model: components return Node values and compose with rsx!
- Reactive primitives: create_signal, create_computed, create_effect, create_resource
- Rendering targets: DOM rendering for WASM, plus server-side rendering through momenta-ssr
- Routing: client-side routing through momenta-router
- Macros: procedural macros for components and JSX-like syntax through momenta-macros

Workspace crates
- momenta: public framework surface and prelude
- momenta-core: reactive core and node primitives
- momenta-dom: DOM renderer for browser apps
- momenta-router: routing primitives and router context
- momenta-ssr: buffered, chunked, and hydratable SSR helpers
- momenta-macros: framework macros including component support

Common documentation entry points
- /getting-started
- /philosophy
- /rsx
- /signals
- /computed-signals
- /effects
- /resources
- /components
- /classes
- /when
- /lists
- /routing
- /performance
- /ssr
- /deployment
- /examples

Canonical positioning
Momenta aims to provide a Rust-first developer experience for building interactive interfaces with predictable fine-grained updates, type-safe components, and straightforward SSR support.
